visualGoal: Make a buyer treat the mat as safety gear, not a checkout rounding error, and steer them to the right joint-forgiving or realism pick for their floor. Real hitting-mat, landing-turf, and netting product proof throughout; no procedural art.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Realism-ceiling gel divot mat, intentionally text-only (gearpick carries no image) because no verified photo. Source real product photography before featuring it as a body visual; price left as check-current.
Modular base + 3 swappable strips (Standard/Foam Divot/Gel Divot), text-only gearpick until real photo sourced. Confirm current base + per-strip pricing.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic dark cinematic golf simulator bay, a clubhead striking a golf ball off a premium fairway-green hitting mat with a faint spark of impact, warm gold rim light on deep green walls, open product-overlay space beside the mat for a real hitting-mat product, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, warped clubhead, impossible geometry, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Which mat: joints or realism?
content: Joints first: Fiberbuilt (~95% shock) or SIGPRO Softy (softest, swappable strip)
Realism value: Country Club Elite (~$479-$600, real divot feel)
Avoid: sub-$150 foam pad on a hard floor
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, golf greens/golds, real mat photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Mat height vs your launch monitor
content: Tall mat raises ball above sensor reference plane
Tall mat on bare slab raises ball above your feet = distorted club path
Measure the FINISHED floor: subfloor + turf + mat, stand level
infographicPrompts.style: Side-elevation diagram with measurement callouts, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult golf curator Nina Brooks kneeling beside a premium hitting mat in a clean home simulator bay, one hand on the turf, confident equipment-expert energy, product overlay space beside her, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

openQuestions: Source verified product photography for TrueStrike and Carl's HotShot so they can move from text-only gearpicks to body visuals?
Confirm current TrueStrike US pricing and Carl's HotShot base + per-strip pricing (both left as check-current)?
Confirm CCE 4x5 price (often near $599) and SIGPRO Softy 4x10 upcharge before tightening price language?
